description: Fix updateCachedTeamSlug() crashing with TypeError when called with a malformed URL, as it is called from the onNavigate lifecycle hook where uncaught errors terminate the navigation handler.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/vercel && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Wrap new URL() in updateCachedTeamSlug() with try-catch
userStories.description: In plugins/vercel/src/vercel-api.ts, updateCachedTeamSlug() at line 66 calls `new URL(url).pathname` without a try-catch. If the url argument is malformed or not a valid URL string (e.g., during SPA navigation events that fire before the URL is fully formed), this throws a TypeError. Since updateCachedTeamSlug() is called from onNavigate, the unhandled TypeError crashes the adapter's navigation handler. Add a try-catch to gracefully handle invalid URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: new URL(url) in updateCachedTeamSlug() is wrapped in a try-catch
If the URL is invalid, the function returns early without modifying the auth cache
Valid URLs are still processed correctly (teamSlug extracted and cached updated)
